role_title: Senior Director, Strategy and Growth Initiatives

location.office_time_required: four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices

level_signals: 10+ years of experience in strategy/management consulting, corporate strategy, or operating roles
Comp range $253,000–$350,000 base, consistent with Senior Director at a late-stage fintech
Partners with senior leaders to shape long-term vision

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in strategy/management consulting, corporate strategy, or operating roles within financial services or fintech
Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive alignment across complex organizationsx
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, including financial modeling and data-driven decision making
Experience leading and managing project teams and driving initiatives end-to-end
Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate insights into clear recommendations

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep experience in fintech/financial services and/or hands-on experience with data analysis, market research, or product analytics (preferred)

scope.surface: Company-wide long-term strategy and growth initiatives across Product, Marketing, Analytics, and other functions
scope.business_model: B2C fintech / financial technology platform (banking services via partner banks)
primary_metrics_inferred: Execution success rate of strategic initiatives
core_responsibilities: Identify and lead high-impact strategic initiatives that drive long-term growth and business durability
Analyze market, competitor, and consumer trends to inform company strategy and priorities
Partner closely with senior leaders to shape long-term vision and drive alignment across the organization
Translate strategy into actionable plans and ensure initiatives are executed across cross-functional teams
Develop deep analytical insights and research to support decision-making and prioritization
Influence decisions and drive accountability across Product, Marketing, Analytics, and other functions
Lead complex problem-solving efforts and guide project teams to deliver measurable business outcomes

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and competitive ($253K–$350K base)
Clear articulation of responsibilities and cross-functional scope
Flexible remote program noted alongside hybrid policy
Strong benefits package including 24 weeks parental leave, 401k match, and wellness stipend
Fintech domain with meaningful mission (financial progress for everyday Americans)
red_flags: JD tag '#LI-Onsite' conflicts with the body text describing a hybrid (4 days in office) plus a 'fully remote program' — actual work arrangement is ambiguous; should be confirmed with recruiter
The phrase 'complex organizationsx' in a must-have requirement appears to be a typo, suggesting the JD may not have been carefully proofread
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned at all
Cities for the role are not explicitly named in the body (only inferred from SF-specific ordinance references in EEO section)

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, comp range, and stakeholder scope)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'leading and managing project teams' language)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (inferred from San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance reference in EEO section; no city explicitly named for the role)
ambiguities: Location type: JD tag '#LI-Onsite' suggests on-site, but the benefits section describes a hybrid policy of 4 days/week in office plus Fridays remote, AND references a 'fully remote program.' Treated as hybrid here, but fully-remote eligibility is unclear.

The fintech/financial services experience and data analysis requirement is listed as '(preferred)' at the end of an otherwise hard-requirement paragraph, making it ambiguous whether it is a true nice-to-have or partially required. Classified as nice-to-have based on the '(preferred)' qualifier.
No specific office cities are listed in the role description; San Francisco is inferred from EEO ordinance references.
